en.extract:
The black-capped woodland warbler (Phylloscopus herberti) is a leaf warbler species in the family Phylloscopidae; it was formerly placed in the "Old World warbler" assemblage.
It is native to the Western High Plateau, Bioko and Equatorial Guinea. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

The '''black-capped woodland warbler''' ('''''Phylloscopus herberti''''') is a [[leaf warbler]] [[species]] in the [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Phylloscopidae]]; it was formerly placed in the "[[Old World warbler]]" assemblage.

It is native to the [[Western High Plateau]], [[Bioko]] and [[Equatorial Guinea]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.
